Class Global

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.Global
public class Global extends Object
Global access to services via the current application context.

This is a convenience utility for deep-framework code or user scripts that don't want to pass AppContext explicitly.

The application context created using DefaultAppContext.createWithDefaults() contains the following default components:

  • ScheduledExecutorService (lazy): creates threads that inherit the AppContext of the creator thread so you can use it instead of creating threads manually;
  • EventDispatcher: the global event dispatcher;
  • Cleaner (lazy): can be used to clean up resources once an object is no longer used;
  • AudioManager (lazy, not in headless): loads & plays sounds;
  • SoundDecoder (not in headless): decodes sound files (mostly used by the audio manager);
  • GraphicsManager (lazy, not in headless): manages rendering contexts;
  • RendererRegistry (not in headless): provides renderers for objects (mostly used by the graphics manager);
  • FontProvider (not in headless): provides TTF fonts (mostly used by the graphics manager).

Example: 

   AudioManager audioManager = Global.get(AudioManager.class);
   audioManager.loadSound(Path.of("ambient.ogg"));
   // ...
Another example: 
    Global.when(StartEvent.class, e -> {
        System.out.println("Green flag clicked!");
        Stage stage = e.getStage();
        // prepare for execution (only preparation code must be here!)
    });
See Also:
  • AppContext
  • AppContextHolder

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    CompletableFuture<Void>
    fire(Event event)
    Fires the given event on the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context), returning immediately.
    void
    fireAndWait(Event event)
    Fires the given event on the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context), and waits until all event listeners finish executing.
    <T> T
    get(Class<T> type)
    Gets a component instance from the global context by the given type.
    <T> Collection<T>
    getAll(Class<T> type)
    Gets all component instances from the global context by the given type.
    ListenerRemover
    registerEventListener(Object o)
    The behaviour of this method depends on the type of the given object: if the given object is a Class object: registers all static methods annotated with @EventListener in the class represented by the object as event listeners in the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context); if the given object is not a Class object: registers all instance methods annotated with @EventListener in the class of the object as event listeners in the global event dispatcher.
    <E extends Event>
    ListenerRemover
    when(Class<E> eventClass, Consumer<? super E> listener)
    Adds a listener to the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context).

  • Constructor Details

    • Global

      public Global()

  • Method Details

    • get

      public <T> T get(Class<T> type)
      Gets a component instance from the global context by the given type.

      This is a shortcut for AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().get(type).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type that was used to register the component (usually an interface type)
      Returns:
      the component instance
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      IllegalArgumentException - if no components were registered under the given type
      NullPointerException - if type is null, or if there's a lazy component registered under that type in the global context and its factory function returns null

    • getAll

      public <T> Collection<T> getAll(Class<T> type)
      Gets all component instances from the global context by the given type.

      This is a shortcut for AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().getAll(type).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the common supertype of the components
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type)
      Returns:
      all components registered under that type
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      IllegalArgumentException - if no components were registered under the given type
      NullPointerException - if type is null, or if there's a lazy component registered under that type in the global context and its factory function returns null

    • when

      public <E extends Event> ListenerRemover when(Class<E> eventClass, Consumer<? super E> listener)
      Adds a listener to the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context).

      This is a shortcut for:
      AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().get(EventDispatcher.class).addListener(...).

      Type Parameters:
      E - the type of event
      Parameters:
      eventClass - the type of the event
      listener - the listener to add
      Returns:
      an object that can be used to remove the listener
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      See Also:
      • EventDispatcher.addListener(Class, Consumer)

    • registerEventListener

      public ListenerRemover registerEventListener(Object o)
      The behaviour of this method depends on the type of the given object:
      1. if the given object is a Class object: registers all static methods annotated with @EventListener in the class represented by the object as event listeners in the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context);
      2. if the given object is not a Class object: registers all instance methods annotated with @EventListener in the class of the object as event listeners in the global event dispatcher.

      This is a shortcut for:
      AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().get(EventDispatcher.class).register(...).

      Parameters:
      o - the object
      Returns:
      an object that can be used to remove the listeners
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      IllegalArgumentException - if a method annotated with @EventListener has invalid arguments
      InaccessibleObjectException - if a method could not be reflectively accessed for any reason
      NullPointerException - if the object is null (implementation-dependent)
      See Also:
      • EventDispatcher.register(Object)
      • EventListener

    • fire

      public CompletableFuture<Void> fire(Event event)
      Fires the given event on the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context), returning immediately.

      This is a shortcut for:
      AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().get(EventDispatcher.class).fireEvent(...);

      Parameters:
      event - the event to fire
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture that completes when all event handlers are done
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      NullPointerException - if the given event is null (implementation-dependent)
      See Also:
      • EventDispatcher.fireEvent(Event)

    • fireAndWait

      public void fireAndWait(Event event)
      Fires the given event on the global event dispatcher (the event dispatcher registered in the global context), and waits until all event listeners finish executing.

      This is a shortcut for:
      AppContextHolder.getContextOrThrow().get(EventDispatcher.class).fireEventAndWait(...);

      Parameters:
      event - the event to fire
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if there is no context for the current thread
      NullPointerException - if the given event is null (implementation-dependent)
      See Also:
      • EventDispatcher.fireEventAndWait(Event)
